title: find_mesopotamian_parallel data payload
description:
Structured payload for the v0.8 `find_mesopotamian_parallel` tool — the cross-Mesopotamian-internal sibling to v0.6's `find_antediluvian_parallel`. Returns curated parallels between Mesopotamian texts/figures (Sumerian↔Akkadian, Akkadian↔Ugaritic, Hurrian↔Akkadian, etc.) WITHOUT requiring a Jewish/Christian biblical passage as the entry-point.

Discipline: same as v0.6 — named scholarly attribution REQUIRED (`scholarly_attribution.minItems: 1`). No scholar, no result. Difference from v0.6: the query is keyed on Mesopotamian-internal axes (deity, theme, tradition-pair, text) rather than a Jewish passage.

The v0.8 dataset ships with 6 parallels validated by the Discovery Engine v0.7 pipeline — they are cross-Mesopotamian-internal and therefore don't fit v0.6's Jewish-passage query structure. Includes the foundational Chaoskampf parallel (Gunkel 1895), the Ninurta→Marduk substitution thesis (Lambert 1986), the Hannahanna↔Bēlet-ilī Hurrian-Hittite↔Mesopotamian mother-goddess equivalence, named-authorship tradition (Helle 2019/2023), the Lagash↔SKL king-list dissent, and the Mesopotamian descent-and-ascent paired motif (Annus 2016).

Provenance is `source: local`, `endpoint: local:mesopotamianParallelsIndex`.

query.description: Echo of the input query, normalized. All filters are AND-combined.

deity_name.description: Filter results that mention this deity. Matches against the per-result `deities[]` field (case-insensitive substring). E.g. 'Marduk', 'Inanna', 'Bēlet-ilī'.

theme.description: Filter by theme tag. Matches against per-result `themes[]`. Vocabulary: `chaoskampf`, `cosmogonic_combat`, `divine_substitution`, `mother_goddess`, `named_authorship`, `king_list_dissent`, `descent_ascent`, `cosmological_paired_motif`, `boundary_crossing`, `succession`, `divine_kingship_via_combat`.

tradition_pair.description: Filter by tradition pair, e.g. `akkadian↔ugaritic`, `sumerian↔akkadian`, `hurrian↔akkadian`, `akkadian↔akkadian`, `sumerian↔sumerian`. Order-insensitive — `akkadian↔ugaritic` matches `ugaritic↔akkadian`.

text_name.description: Filter by text name. Matches against per-result `texts[]` (case-insensitive substring). E.g. 'Enūma Eliš', 'Baal Cycle', 'Erra Epic', 'lugal-e'.

results.description: Matching parallels. Order: by correspondence_strength (strong > moderate > weak) then by curated insertion order.

MesopotamianParallel.description: Single curated Mesopotamian-internal parallel. Same shape conventions as ParallelCandidate in find_antediluvian_parallel; differences are: (1) no Jewish-passage query keying; (2) entity_a and entity_b are both Mesopotamian/ANE; (3) themes and deities arrays for queryability.

parallel_type.description: Same vocabulary as v0.6 plus `logographic` for the case where two deities are conflated by shared Sumerogram (e.g. DINGIR.MAḪ for Hannahanna and Bēlet-ilī).

themes.description: Theme tags for queryability. Use the controlled vocabulary from `query.theme`.

deities.description: Deities mentioned in either entity, for queryability. Canonical Akkadian form preferred.

texts.description: Texts mentioned in either entity, for queryability.

scholarly_attribution.description: REQUIRED. Named publications establishing the parallel.

transmission_hypothesis.description: Mechanism by which the parallel arose. `direct_borrowing` = one text consciously adapted from the other (e.g. Marduk-Tiamat from Ninurta-Asag per Lambert 1986); `syncretism` = same deity under different names/cultures (e.g. Hannahanna ≈ Bēlet-ilī).

discovery_origin.description: If this parallel originated from the Discovery Engine, this block captures the provenance back to discoveredCandidates.json.

text.description: Description of the entity. E.g. 'Marduk\'s combat with Tiamat in Enūma Eliš IV.93-104.'
